stem: In triangle XYZ, angle Y is a right angle, point P lies on XZ, and point Q lies on YZ such that PQ is parallel to XY. If the measure of angle XZY is 63°, what is the measure, in degrees, of angle XPQ?
explanation: Because PQ is parallel to XY and angle Y is right, angle ZQP is also right, so in triangle ZQP angle ZPQ = 90 − 63 = 27°. Angle XPQ is supplementary to angle ZPQ, so it measures 180 − 27 = 153°.
